activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct third-person form.
activities.prompt: Eli have to open the shop at eight.

evaluator.answer: Eli has to open the shop at eight

activities.explanation: La tercera persona singular usa «has to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the incorrect “to”.
activities.prompt: You must to keep the medicine away from children.

evaluator.answer: You must keep the medicine away from children

activities.explanation: «Must» va seguido de infinitivo sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence without adding an ending to the modal.
activities.prompt: Every visitor musts show a ticket.

evaluator.answer: Every visitor must show a ticket

activities.explanation: Los verbos modales no añaden «-s».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct plural form.
activities.prompt: The volunteers has to arrive by nine.

evaluator.answer: The volunteers have to arrive by nine

activities.explanation: El sujeto plural «volunteers» usa «have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct modal structure.
activities.prompt: We do must check the address again.

evaluator.answer: We must check the address again

activities.explanation: «Must» no necesita el auxiliar «do» en afirmativa.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete question with the correct base form.
activities.prompt: Does Finn has to bring his own laptop?

evaluator.answer: Does Finn have to bring his own laptop

activities.explanation: Después de «Does» usamos «have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete question with “Do”.
activities.prompt: Have customers to book an appointment?

evaluator.answer: Do customers have to book an appointment

activities.explanation: La pregunta estándar usa «Do customers have to ...?».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct past obligation form.
activities.prompt: We musted leave the campsite when the river rose.

evaluator.answer: We had to leave the campsite when the river rose

activities.explanation: «Musted» no existe; usamos «had to» para obligación pasada.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct form after “didn't”.
activities.prompt: I didn't had to pay for the replacement card.

evaluator.answer: I didn't have to pay for the replacement card

activities.explanation: Después de «didn't» usamos la forma base «have».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct future obligation form.
activities.prompt: Next week, Theo will must work from the regional office.

evaluator.answer: Next week, Theo will have to work from the regional office

activities.explanation: Después de «will» expresamos obligación con «have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete rule so it expresses prohibition.
activities.prompt: You don't must enter the laboratory without a supervisor.

evaluator.answer: You mustn't enter the laboratory without a supervisor

activities.explanation: La negativa del modal de prohibición es «mustn't».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence so it means that bringing a cable is unnecessary.
activities.prompt: You mustn't bring a charging cable; each desk already has one.

evaluator.answer: You don't have to bring a charging cable; each desk already has one

activities.explanation: El cable disponible elimina la necesidad; no prohíbe llevar otro.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence so it expresses a prohibition.
activities.prompt: Climbers don't have to cross the barrier marked “Danger”.

evaluator.answer: Climbers mustn't cross the barrier marked “Danger”

activities.explanation: Una barrera de peligro exige «mustn't», no una forma opcional.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete future sentence with the correct negative structure.
activities.prompt: After the software update, users don't will have to enter the code twice.

evaluator.answer: After the software update, users won't have to enter the code twice

activities.explanation: La ausencia de necesidad futura se forma con «won't have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete past question with the base form.
activities.prompt: Did the driver had to report the incident?

evaluator.answer: Did the driver have to report the incident

activities.explanation: Después de «Did» usamos «have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete indirect question without inversion.
activities.prompt: Can you tell me whether do guests have to remove their shoes?

evaluator.answer: Can you tell me whether guests have to remove their shoes

activities.explanation: La pregunta indirecta mantiene el orden afirmativo: «whether guests have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct singular negative form.
activities.prompt: Mara don't have to attend the rehearsal on Sunday.

evaluator.answer: Mara doesn't have to attend the rehearsal on Sunday

activities.explanation: El sujeto singular usa «doesn't have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct practical-necessity form.
activities.prompt: The damaged cable needs be replaced before the machine can be used.

evaluator.answer: The damaged cable needs to be replaced before the machine can be used

activities.explanation: El patrón correcto es «needs to be replaced».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct form after “will”.
activities.prompt: Each department will has to reduce its energy use.

evaluator.answer: Each department will have to reduce its energy use

activities.explanation: Después de «will» usamos la forma base «have».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence with the correct no-necessity structure.
activities.prompt: We hadn't to reserve seats because the carriage was almost empty.

evaluator.answer: We didn't have to reserve seats because the carriage was almost empty

activities.explanation: La ausencia de necesidad pasada usa «didn't have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence so the online-only rule is a prohibition.
activities.prompt: Applicants don't have to send documents by post; postal applications are not accepted.

evaluator.answer: Applicants mustn't send documents by post; postal applications are not accepted

activities.explanation: Si las solicitudes postales no se aceptan, enviarlas está prohibido.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence so the action is optional rather than forbidden.
activities.prompt: Delegates mustn't stay for the networking dinner; leaving after the final talk is allowed.

evaluator.answer: Delegates don't have to stay for the networking dinner; leaving after the final talk is allowed

activities.explanation: Poder marcharse convierte la cena en opcional: «don't have to stay».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting both obligation forms.
activities.prompt: Last year we must renew the licence monthly; next year we will must renew it quarterly.

evaluator.answer: Last year we had to renew the licence monthly; next year we will have to renew it quarterly

activities.explanation: El pasado usa «had to» y el futuro «will have to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete question, correcting the indirect-question order and verb form.
activities.prompt: Do you know if does each speaker has to provide a biography?

evaluator.answer: Do you know if each speaker has to provide a biography

activities.explanation: La pregunta indirecta usa orden afirmativo y «each speaker has to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting both the prohibition and future no-necessity forms.
activities.prompt: Visitors don't must touch the instruments, but after the demonstration they don't will have to wear gloves.

evaluator.answer: Visitors mustn't touch the instruments, but after the demonstration they won't have to wear gloves

activities.explanation: La prohibición usa «mustn't» y la ausencia de necesidad futura, «won't have to».
